mnemonics: FLYNN: Classify systems by Instruction & Data Streams (SISD, SIMD, MISD, MIMD)
keyPoints: Flynn's Taxonomy is the primary classification system for parallel computers, based on instruction and data streams.
Parallel hardware architectures include shared memory (UMA/NUMA) and distributed memory systems.
Parallel software requires specific programming models (e.g., MPI, OpenMP) to manage communication and synchronization between processors.
commonMistakes: Assuming MISD (Multiple Instruction, Single Data) is a common or practical architecture—it's mostly theoretical.
Confusing shared memory (all processors access a common address space) with distributed memory (each processor has its own private memory).
Thinking that more processors always lead to a linear speedup, ignoring overhead from communication and synchronization.
